content.problemStatement:
Chef has two integer sequences A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã. You should choose N+M‚àí1N+M-1N+M‚àí1 pairs, each in the form (Ax,By)(A_x, B_y)(Ax‚Äã,By‚Äã), such that the sums Ax+ByA_x + B_yAx‚Äã+By‚Äã are all pairwise distinct.

It is guaranteed that under the given constraints, a solution always exists. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
constraints: A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: Print N+M‚àí1N+M-1N+M‚àí1 lines. Each of these lines should contain two space-separated integers xxx and yyy denoting that you chose a pair (Ax+1,By+1)(A_{x+1}, B_{y+1})(Ax+1‚Äã,By+1‚Äã).
